content.problemStatement:
Little Bear had just come back from school. He was very hungry that time and wanted at least K units of honey to satisfy his hunger. He immediately went to a rectangular field of size N x M to collect some honey. He wanted to collect honey only
in a special sub-rectangle which has size S x T where S<=A and T<=B, for some fixed A and B. He has Q queries, each query contains A, B. For each query, he wanted to know how many special sub-rectangles contain at least K units of honey.
content.inputFormat: First line contains three integers N, M, and K. The next N lines contain M characters ('H' or '.'). The next line contains an integer Q. The next Q lines contain two integers A and B.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print the number of special sub-rectangles which contain at least K units of honey.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation for 2nd query: The possibilities are: 1. Size 2 x 3, 2. Size 2 x 3, 3. Size 2 x 3, 4. Size 2 x 2
Explanation for query: The possibilities are: 1. Size 2 x 2, 2. Size 2 x 2
Explanation for query: The possibilities are: 1. Size 1 x 1, 2. Size 1 x 1

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by using a prefix sum array to store the cumulative sum of honey in the field. For each query, we iterate over all possible sub-rectangles of size A x B and check if the sum of honey in the sub-rectangle is greater than or equal to K. We use the prefix sum array to calculate the sum of honey in each sub-rectangle in O(1) time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(Q * N * M) per test case, O(T * Q * N * M) total. Precomputation is done once to calculate the prefix sum array.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * M) — the dominant structure is the prefix sum array.
